title: apicover in-process gate: thread context.Context into apicover.Run so the audit's apicoverTimeout actually bounds the measurement (wedge-resistance regression from one-binary S1)
summary: Surfaced by go-reviewer on one-binary S1 (folding apicover from a subprocess to an in-process call). Before S1, apicoverEnforceChangedDefault (ciparity.go) ran `bin/apicover -enforce` through sysexec (exec.CommandContext), so the 8-minute apicoverTimeout ctx SIGKILL'd any hang. After S1, `apicover.Run(Config, io.Writer)` is a plain synchronous call with no ctx parameter: Enumerate's os.ReadFile + go/parser AST walk over the touched packages has no cancellation hook, so a stalled/network-mounted worktree FS or a pathological source file blocks the whole audit gate indefinitely instead of timing out. Low real-world likelihood (small already-checked-out local packages, no network) but a genuine loss of the wedge-resistance this codebase takes seriously (cf. driver_quota_hang_wedge). ROOT-CAUSE FIX (not a workaround): add a ctx-aware entry to internal/apicover — e.g. RunContext(ctx, Config, io.Writer) that checks ctx.Done() between package enumerations (or threads ctx into Enumerate's file reads), keeping Run(Config, io.Writer) as a context.Background() shim for the CLI/Main path. Then ciparity.go passes its existing ctx (ciparity.go ~line 251) into RunContext so apicoverTimeout binds again. Belt-and-suspenders alternative if per-package granularity is enough: run apicover.Run in a goroutine and select on ctx.Done() in ciparity.go, but prefer real ctx threading so a single pathological file can't wedge a whole package's parse.
acceptance: internal/apicover exposes a context-aware measurement entry (RunContext or equivalent) that returns promptly when the ctx is cancelled mid-enumeration; Run(Config,io.Writer) preserved as a Background shim (CLI/Main byte-parity unchanged)

apicoverEnforceChangedDefault threads its apicoverTimeout ctx into the in-process call; a regression test proves a cancelled/expired ctx aborts the gate instead of hanging (e.g. table with a pre-cancelled ctx -> gate returns an error/offender, not a block)
go test -race ./internal/apicover/... ./internal/phases/audit/... PASS; apicover clean; evolve apicover byte-parity with standalone unchanged
source: go-reviewer MEDIUM finding on one-binary S1 re-review 2026-07-14
